definition: China's 85-90% concentration in rare earth processing constitutes the highest-severity supply chain vulnerability for the energy transition, with an HHI of 7,200-8,100 (roughly 2x OPEC 1973 at 3,000-3,500) and a 5-10 year unclosable window before non-Chinese capacity reaches meaningful scale.
evidence: Non-Chinese capacity (Lynas + MP Materials) at <15% of Chinese processing
Sequential weaponization pattern: Ga/Ge → graphite → antimony → processing tech bans
Near-zero substitutability for EV permanent magnet motors on 3-5 year horizons
Non-China processing reaching 25-30% share is 2030-2032 at earliest
Prices spiked then normalized for Ga/Ge suggesting inventory buffers limit demonstrated impact
open_questions: Will China fully weaponize rare earth leverage or maintain restrained escalation?
Can ferrite magnets and induction motors substitute for permanent magnets at scale?
How large are inventory buffers relative to potential supply disruption duration?
